betterquesting:10.desc:8:
The §6Observatory§f is a 5×5×5 multiblock (TileObservatory.java) that scans the overworld and nearby dimensions for §6unexplored planets§f, generating the §6Planet ID Chips§f (advancedrocketry:planetidchip) that gate the §6Astrobody Data Processor§f (10020) and every subsequent mission. It is the only way to discover new star systems in the AR progression.

§6Production Path (libVulpes + TileObservatory.java:36)§f:
- Structure: 5×5×5 hollow box of §6Structure Blocks§f; the center column holds three §6Lens Blocks§f (or §6Glass§f) at three elevations; the bottom layer has a §6Motor§f capped with §6Structure Tower§f blocks; a §6Control Circuit Board§f (advancedrocketry:ic:3) marks the controller
- Built via §6Crafting Table§f: 18x §6Glass Pane§f (any color, minecraft:stained_glass_pane:0-15) and one §6Structure Tower§f cap, registered through `RecipesMachine.getInstance()` for the observatory class
- Power: 100 RF/t while scanning
- The observatory produces §6Planet ID Chips§f via GUI button §6Scan Planet§f; chips must be inserted into an §6Astrobody Data Processor§f to research atmosphere, mass, and distance (TileAstrobodyDataProcessor.java)

The observatory is the foundation of the entire §6exploration§f questline. It requires the §6Structure Tower§f (10001) and §6Lens Block§f (10013) prereqs and unlocks the §6Astrobody Data Processor§f (10020) chain. Once a planet is scanned and researched, you can build a §6Rocket Assembler§f (10022) and fly to it.

§eTip§f: Replace one of the three §6Lens Blocks§f in the column with a regular §6Glass Block§f to cheapen the build; the observatory still completes as long as at least two of the three lens positions use §6Lens Blocks§f. The glass panes must be at least one of the standard §6minecraft:glass_pane§f variants.
